mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks by providing a standardized platform. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and the requirement for specific resource allocations.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to a directory in your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kagenti cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kagenti cluster on your Kubernetes environment with the specified version and node count.

```bash

kagenti cluster create --version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as per your requirements.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kagenti cluster.

```bash

kagenti kubeconfig set-context --cluster kagenti-cluster --user kagenti-user

```

> This command sets the context for your Kubernetes configuration.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kagenti cluster to ensure they are all running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the Kagenti deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes.

```bash

kagenti cluster delete --name kagenti-cluster

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command deletes the Kagenti cluster.
> This command removes the namespace associated with Kagenti.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your current configuration. This step will upgrade your Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

kagenti upgrade --version v0.5.1

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backup current resources.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kagenti cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified cluster does not exist or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster name and ensure it exists using 'kagenti cluster list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command-line tool is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the official Kubernetes installation guide.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to create node'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to create the requested nodes.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements: 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM per node.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig context is not set correctly or the cluster is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig context with 'kubectl config current-context' and ensure the cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved security features, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in the 'Ready' state, indicating that your Kagenti cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up with the required resources.
